title: Setup Findings Promotion First Slice
goal: Add one compact setup/jumpstart findings promotion contract so Agentic Workspace can accept useful agent-produced analysis input without becoming its own analysis framework.
non_goals: Require all agents to emit the same analysis output beyond one optional compact artifact shape.
Auto-promote every setup finding into checked-in state.
Expand into memory-wide usefulness or declarative-schema extraction in this slice.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Setup and jumpstart can accept agent-produced findings, preserve only the high-value ones, and route them into durable repo state without broad new workflow overhead.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Workspace setup can now consume one compact external findings artifact and distinguish promotable setup findings from transient analysis residue.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: broader finding classes beyond repo-friction evidence and planning candidates.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The contract stays quiet only if routing remains advisory and bounded; auto-writing planning or memory state from setup input would be too heavy for the first slice.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: `agentic-workspace setup` exposes the contract, accepts an optional findings artifact, and reports promotable versus transient findings while `agentic-workspace report` consumes the repo-friction evidence class.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: none beyond ordinary future setup/jumpstart dogfooding in other repos.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Return to the roadmap queue after archiving this tranche; do not widen setup findings into a generic analyzer.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Add one optional setup findings input plus a compact promotion contract for the first two finding classes.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep setup bounded, keep the contract optional, and do not let the workspace layer own the analysis method itself.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: The exact file name and payload field names, which two finding classes land first, and the narrowest durable route for each class.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The best-looking change would auto-write planning or memory state, require a general query language, or widen setup into a broad analysis framework.

active_milestone.scope: define the contract, accept one optional artifact, route repo-friction evidence plus planning candidates, and dogfood it in this repo.

immediate_next_action: Archive this completed slice, close issue `#127`, and advance the roadmap to the declarative contract inventory lane.
invariants: The workspace layer accepts findings input but does not own the analysis mechanism.
Promotion remains explicit and advisory; transient findings stay transient.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: The first artifact path should stay optional and compact.
The first promoted classes should stay narrow and easy to route.
Repo-friction evidence may become durable report input; planning candidates may become explicit promotion guidance without auto-writing planning state.
open_questions_to_close: Which compact artifact path is the quietest default for setup/jumpstart findings?
What exact fields are minimally necessary to make a finding routable without over-specifying agent internals?
completion_criteria: `agentic-workspace setup --target ./repo --format json` exposes a compact setup-findings promotion contract and optional external input handling.
One optional checked-in artifact shape is documented and accepted.
Repo-friction evidence from that artifact becomes queryable through shared reporting.
Planning candidates from that artifact are surfaced as explicit promotion guidance rather than silent active work.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Added one compact setup findings promotion contract, accepted the optional `tools/setup-findings.json` artifact through `agentic-workspace setup`, and routed promotable repo-friction evidence into shared reporting while keeping planning candidates as explicit promotion guidance.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: `uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py`; repo-local dogfood with a transient `tools/setup-findings.json` artifact confirmed `SETUP_STATUS=loaded`, `SETUP_REPO_FRICTION=1`, and `SETUP_PLANNING=1`.
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: `ROADMAP.md` next candidate `Declarative contract inventory and schemas`
execution_summary.resume from: No further action in this plan; return to the next roadmap lane when a new bounded slice is promoted.

drift_log: 2026-04-17: Promoted roadmap issue `#127` into an active first-slice execplan.
2026-04-17: Landed the setup findings promotion contract, dogfooded it with a transient repo-local artifact, and prepared the plan for archive.
